title: Repository: /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public Task: Review Kernel 4-pane implementation approach against ~/.codex/docs/kernel-4pane-spec.md. Focus only on truthful state surfaces already present in repo and identify minimal viable architecture and risks. Do not edit files. Return concise...
summary_text: ### Findings against `~/.codex/docs/kernel-4pane-spec.md` 1. **Spec-defined 4-pane state files are not present in repo** - No canonical 4-pane runtime files from the spec are written by the implemented scripts (e.g. `~/.codex/state/runs/<run-id>/state.json`, `kernel-lane-runs.jsonl`, `kernel-4pane-active.json`, `kernel-lane-shape.jsonl` style paths were not found as implemented sinks). 2. **Kernel currently uses a different authoritative state model** - Active state is centralized around: - [kernel bootstrap receipt](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-bootstrap-receipt.sh) - [runtime ledger](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-ledger.sh) - [glm run state](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-glm-run-state.sh) - [runtime status surface](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-status-surface.sh) - [compact artifact](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-compact-artifact.sh) - Path authority is defined in [kernel-state-paths.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-state-paths.sh), not the spec’s `~/.codex/state` defaults. 3. **Tmux layout does not match 4-pane design contract** - Existing launch/recovery flow uses 4 windows (`main`, `logs`, `review`, `ops`) and a fugue/codex runtime process, not the spec’s dedicated 4-pane health/ship/lanes session model. 4. **No dedicated ship monitor automation exists in current 4-pane form** - There is no `kernel-ship.sh`/monitor loop script in repo; shipping appears handled by prompt/guard/recovery contracts and completion workflows, not by 4-pane “ship pane” polling. 5. **Prompt/contract currently expects different primitives** - Contract checks reference bootstrap, lane counts, manifest, and health/degradation controls in [check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh) and contract docs ([schema-v1](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/docs/kernel/interfaces/schema-v1.md), [contracts](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/docs/kernel/interfaces/contracts.md)), indicating a different validated surface than the 4-pane spec draft. ### Minimal viable architecture (no-new-source-of-truth) 1. Keep existing canonical state files as authority: - bootstrap receipt + runtime ledger + glm run state + compact artifact. 2. Add a small 4-pane projection layer that reads only those canonical files and writes **no** new durable runtime state. 3. Implement pane renderers that materialize view slices: - lane summary (from receipt), - health (from runtime health script outputs), - ship status (from compact/ledger terminal signals), - logs (existing `tmux` logs tail). 4. Add a compatibility serializer only for 4-pane UI consumers if required, with one-way derivation and explicit source-of-truth annotation. 5. Gate rollout on read-only mode first, then optional migration path if spec consumers require writeback. ### Risks 1. **Dual-truth divergence** if 4-pane files are written independently of ledger/receipt/compact. 2. **Path mismatch risk** between current `~/.config/kernel`-style roots and spec `~/.codex/state`. 3. **Recovery/monitor inconsistency** since current recovery orchestration is window-oriented, not pane-oriented. 4. **Contract drift** if 4-pane introduces state fields not represented in `schema-v1`/`contracts.md`.
